title: Macrobiotic Dongbei Fish Shui Zhu Yu
description: A macrobiotic-friendly, crispy Chinese dish featuring fish, tortillas, and toast, perfect for a sick day. This dish is a cultured twist on traditional Shui Zhu Yu.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together sesame oil, soy sauce, grated ginger, and minced garlic.
Add the cod fish pieces to the bowl and marinate for 10 minutes.
Place the marinated fish on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 12 minutes.
While the fish is baking, toast the whole grain bread cubes until crispy.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil over medium heat and add the chopped scallions.
Add the tortilla strips to the skillet and cook until crispy and golden brown.
To assemble the dish, place the toasted bread cubes at the bottom of a bowl, followed by the crispy tortilla strips, and finally the baked fish.
